title: Zantedeschia oculata
intro: The Zantedeschia aethiopica, also known as Calla Lily or Zantedeschia oculata is not a commonly known species however the  Zantedeschia aethiopica is a well-documented plant. The Zantedeschia aethiopica is a flowering plant species in the Arum family, native to southern Africa. This plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of infections and injuries. The bioactive constituents of Zantedeschia aethiopica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are often extracted and used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Zantedeschia oculata is classified as a member of the family Arumaceae and belongs to the genus Zantedeschia. It is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. Within the Zantedeschia genus, Z. oculata is a distinct species. Zantedeschia oculata is further grouped into the tribe Colletocene, which includes several other species of flowering plants. As a member of the Arumaceae family, Z. oculata shares characteristics with other plants in the same family, such as the calla lily.
uses: Zantedeschia oculata, also known as the eyed arum lily or golden arum lily, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some African cultures, Zantedeschia oculata is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. However, its use in medicine should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.

parts: Zantedeschia oculata, also known as the Golden Calla or Eyeball Calla, has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in South Africa where it is native.
